data.title: What Is Tesla's (TSLA) True Value? Bullish Analyst Weighs In
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA) may not be valued like a typical car company, according to Benchmark analyst Mickey Legg. He reiterated a Buy rating and a $475 price target, implying about 11% upside from current levels. Legg argued the company is moving toward a platform centered on software, self-driving technology, and artificial intelligence rather than traditional vehicle sales.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

From a valuation standpoint, Tesla has a market capitalization of about $1.57 trillion, while its enterprise value stands near $1.41 trillion. Despite this already large size, the stock trades at much higher multiples than most automakers. Tesla's forward price-to-earnings ratio is roughly 394x, far above the sector median near 18, suggesting investors are paying a significant premium for future growth.

Tesla Prioritizes Growth over Short-Term Profit

Legg said Tesla is choosing to reinvest in its future instead of maximizing short-term profits. The company plans to increase spending on autonomy, AI, robotics, and its energy infrastructure. Because of this shift, he believes Tesla should no longer be viewed only as an automaker.

Tesla's recent fourth-quarter results showed stable margins, growth in the energy business, and strong cash generation. The company also reported earnings per share of $0.50 and revenue of $24.90 billion, both above Wall Street expectations. However, the analyst expects 2026 to be a heavier spending year as Tesla builds out these technologies, which could affect its results.

As a result, Tesla's stock in the near term may react more to investor expectations about future projects than to quarterly profit figures. Even if earnings are modest, positive updates on self-driving, AI, or robotics could still push the shares higher. 

Is Tesla a Good Stock to Buy Now?

On Wall Street, analysts have maintained a Neutral stance on Tesla stock. According to TipRanks, TSLA stock has received a Hold consensus rating, with 11 Buys, 11 Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the last three months. The average price target for Tesla shares is $390.14, suggesting a potential downside of 8.90% from the current level.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: RIVN Earnings: Rivian Stock Surges after Better-than-Expected Q4 Results
data.description:
Shares of Rivian Automotive (RIVN) surged in after-hours trading after the firm posted better-than-expected Q4 earnings results. Indeed, the EV maker reported an adjusted loss of $0.70 per share, which beat analysts' consensus forecast of a loss of $0.71 per share. At the same time, revenue came in at $1.29 billion, exceeding expectations of $1.26 billion.

data.title: Warner Bros. Discovery Stock (NASDAQ:WBD) Slips as Paramount Skydance Ramps Up Proxy Fight
data.description:
Entertainment giant Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) is about to find itself in the middle of a serious proxy fight before the meeting to see if shareholders will go for the Netflix (NFLX) deal. In fact, Paramount Skydance (PSKY) has brought in a little help of its own to take on the project. The move did not sit well with Warner investors, though, who sent shares down fractionally in the closing minutes of Thursday's trading.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Reports noted that Paramount is looking to nominate Pentwater Capital Management to the Warner board, a move which would likely help shut down the Netflix (NFLX) bid to take over before it can even begin. More specifically, Paramount wants to talk to Pentwater's CEO, Matt Halbower, to sit on the board, among others.

Halbower, for his part, acknowledged that Paramount had approached him, but noted, "No final determination has been made." Halbower also noted that he would rather not see the whole thing come to a proxy fight to begin with, noting, "If however the Warner Brothers Board fails to engage with Paramount, I would consider that to be a serious breach of the Board's duty owed to shareholders which would make it more probable that I would appear on Paramount's slate."

The Biggest Threat?

While Paramount seems to be putting on a good show as the biggest threat to the Netflix / Warner merger, there are signs that there is actually a bigger threat than Paramount. In fact, some wonder if Netflix might not be the bigger threat here, as Netflix has already lost over $100 billion in market cap since it started the bidding process to get its hands on Warner.

But the bigger threat here might actually be the government. Or at least a government; there are already signs that the Justice Department and the Federal Trade Commission are at least somewhat looking at the deal, and with bipartisan support in Congress coming out against the deal, it is easy to wonder which government agency might scuttle the deal first. Even European Union lawmakers are getting involved, which suggests substantial support for cutting Netflix off.

Is WBD Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on WBD stock based on four Buys and six Holds assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 174.28% rally in its share price over the past year, the average WBD price target of $28.86 per share implies 2.6% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Ford Hikes Bonuses, Ford Stock (NYSE:F) Gains
data.description:
Good news for employees at legacy automaker Ford (F) emerged, as a report from Reuters revealed that companywide bonuses at Ford were set to be quite a bit higher than last year. In fact, the new companywide bonus is 130%. Investors were just fine with this, and gave Ford a modest boost in share prices in Thursday afternoon's trading.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

A while back, we heard that Ford was cutting bonuses and reallocating weights, putting a particular focus on quality metrics. Ford wanted to improve its overall vehicle quality. On the surface, it may have seemed like Ford did not meet that goal, based on the huge number of recalls we saw in 2025. But Ford brass disagreed, and the metric went up.

Interestingly, the numbers agreed with Ford brass. While we know there was a flood of recalls in the last year, it turns out that one of Ford's key metrics—initial vehicle quality—is the best it has been in years. Initial vehicle quality is a measure of repairs made to vehicles in the first 90 days of ownership. Since many of the recalls were on vehicles that were a year old or so, initial quality held the line, and actually delivered the best numbers Ford has seen in a decade. Thus, the bonus hike kicked in.

The Blame Game

Meanwhile, not everyone will be getting hands on that expanded bonus. This is particularly true for those who lost their jobs recently at a battery factory in Kentucky. That meant 1,600 layoffs, mostly after the Trump administration shuttered subsidies for electric vehicles. Despite this, the workers do not blame President Trump. Rather, they blame Ford itself.

The workers believe that Ford is mostly to blame for getting workers' hopes up and then ultimately dashing them, reports note. Indeed, Ford ignored electric vehicles for years, the reports note, before ultimately being forced by Tesla's (TSLA) runaway success to try and get in on the market. Then, when the subsidies cut out, Ford's effort was largely crushed on the runway. One worker noted, "At the end of the day, whatever the government policy would be, the company made the decision."

Is Ford Stock a Good Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on F stock based on two Buys, 11 Holds and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 48.13% rally in its share price over the past year, the average F price target of $13.95 per share implies 0.35% downside risk.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Why Is Silver Down 10% Today, 2/12/26?
data.description:
Silver (XAGUSD) is plunging on Thursday amid a rout in the stock market as investors take off risk. The downturn in stocks has forced some investors to reduce exposure to precious metals in order to raise capital and meet margin requirements.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"For gold and silver, a great deal of trading remains sentiment and momentum driven," said Saxo Bank commodity strategist Ole Hansen. "On days like these they will struggle."

Silver Pulls Back ahead of Key Inflation Data

Profit-taking is also likely responsible for silver's disappointing performance today amid a 136% rally over the past year. MLIV Macro Strategist Michael Ball added that algo selling could be a driving factor behind the selloff as well.

Silver is set to receive a major catalyst when the Bureau of Labor Statistics releases January's Consumer Price Index (CPI), which tracks inflation, on Friday morning. A higher-than-expected reading could benefit silver, as it is viewed as a hedge against inflation and a store of value when purchasing power declines.

data.title: Nova Lake Die Sizes Leak Sends Intel Stock (NASDAQ:INTC) Sliding
data.description:
Some interesting news slipped out recently about chip stock Intel (INTC), specifically about the Nova Lake processor lineup. Intel has a lot riding on the Nova Lake processor, which makes the fact that a leak emerged about these chips all the more unsettling. The news that slipped out focused on die sizes, and gave an advance look at the Nova Lake beyond what we have heard so far. Investors were not pleased, though, as evidenced by a nearly 3% loss in Thursday afternoon's trading.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The new word suggests that the Nova Lake line will have eight Coyote Cove P-cores along with 32 Arctic Wolf E-cores. That means around 110 square millimeters, which is quite a step from Nova Lake's predecessor. That processor measured about 150 square millimeters. That, coupled with the fact that the Nova Lake will be made using TSMC's (TSM) N2 process, means that the Nova Lake line will likely not be inexpensive to make.

It will help that most laptop Nova Lake processors will be made in-house at Intel's Arizona fab. And given that laptop processors outsell desktop processors by roughly seven to three these days, the costs may not be all that egregious after all. Still, knowing what we know so far suggests that higher prices than expected may show up later.

Wedbush Likes It

New reports suggest a reason to like Intel beyond that, though, as Intel is looking to put a $100 million commitment into SambaNova Systems. This might not sound like a great idea on the surface, given how aggressively Intel has been pinching pennies of late. But Wedbush analysts suggest that this could be the start of something truly impressive.

Reports note that Wedbush is calling the move a "…meaningful step in reshaping Intel's role in high-end compute." Given that a lot of computing power is moving to artificial intelligence, and SambaNova has something of a presence therein, Intel's connection to SambaNova could fuel substantial advancement in the higher-end computing sector. The fact that Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan has a personal connection to SambaNova, however, may taint the deal a bit.

Is Intel a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on INTC stock based on eight Buys, 20 Holds and four Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 100.12% rally in its share price over the past year, the average INTC price target of $48.32 per share implies 3.38% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: "One of My Favorites": Boeing Stock (NYSE:BA) Jumps With Jim Cramer's Nod
data.description:
Some might be inherently concerned by the notion that Jim Cramer of CNBC fame likes a stock. After all, there was a mutual fund once that traded on doing the opposite of whatever he recommended. But one of Cramer's latest likes struck a chord, and aerospace giant Boeing (BA) is that like. This was good enough news for investors, who piled in and sent shares up nearly 2% in Thursday afternoon's trading.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Jim Cramer's feelings about Boeing came out plainly on his X feed, as he noted, "Boeing, one of my favorites in the trust: lotta chatter of big wins out of China and Saudi Arabia. Given that they are getting better and better and quicker at making new planes these could take the stock back to $250." All of which is true, of course; we heard about those Saudi wins not too long ago with Saudia, and there were similar signs that China might get involved, even as it struggles mightily to get Comac up and running.

Cramer is not alone here, either; several other analysts have come out on Boeing's side in the last few weeks. UBS, for example, hiked its price target from $275 to $285 per share, holding its Buy rating in place. UBS analysts were particularly pleased with Boeing's improved free cash flow, as well as overall production processes.

The Biggest Building on Earth, By Volume

An unexpected fact about Boeing emerged, meanwhile, as it turns out that Boeing is the current Guinness World Record holder for the largest building by volume. This building, the Everett Factory in Mukilteo, Washington, stands at 472 million cubic feet of space, and features a factory floor containing 98.3 acres. That is roughly the equivalent of 75 football fields.

The building is reportedly so large that it contains its own weather system. A tour actually runs through the building in question, and takes 80 minutes start to finish. This is also where Boeing keeps its Future of Flight gallery as well as several exhibits, like a mock-up of the Destiny module.

Is Boeing a Good Stock to Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on BA stock based on 14 Buys, one Hold and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 27.41% rally in its share price over the past year, the average BA price target of $269.50 per share implies 11.79% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: OpenAI Just Rolled Out Its First AI Model not Powered by Nvidia Chips
data.description:
OpenAI just rolled out a new AI model that's powered by Cerebras chips. Importantly, this is the first time that it has launched a model built with non-Nvidia (NVDA) chips. The model, called GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark, is a lighter and faster version of its newest Codex software, which helps automate coding tasks. While it is less powerful than the full version, it is designed to respond more quickly. That means developers can edit sections of code, run tests, stop the model mid-task, or redirect it to a new assignment without waiting for long computations to finish.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

This move comes as OpenAI continues to increase the number of chip suppliers it works with. Last month, the company signed a deal that was reportedly worth more than $10 billion to use Cerebras hardware in order to deliver faster AI responses. Around the same time, OpenAI reached a major agreement with Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) to deploy 6 gigawatts of AMD graphics processing units over several years. It also agreed to buy custom chips and networking components from Broadcom (AVGO).

Nevertheless, OpenAI made it clear that Nvidia still plays a central role. In a statement, a company spokesperson described the Nvidia relationship as "foundational" and noted that its most advanced AI models are the result of years of close hardware and software collaboration. The company also said that it will continue to rely on Nvidia as the backbone of its training and inference systems, while deliberately expanding its supplier base to include Cerebras, AMD, and Broadcom.

What Is a Good Price for NVDA?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on NVDA stock based on 37 Buys, one Hold, and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average NVDA price target of $260.38 per share implies 37.6% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri
